items.q: Why does historical context matter when reading literature?
items.correct: Texts emerge from and respond to specific historical conditions — context illuminates references, assumptions and arguments that might otherwise be invisible.
items.misconception: Context just means writing about what the author had for breakfast.
items.random_wrong: Context is required because all texts are about history.
items.why: Students treat context as biographical trivia. Useful context is what the text engages with — politics, ideology, genre conventions — not personal detail of the author's life.

items.q: What defines epic poetry as a genre?
items.correct: Long narrative poems on heroic subjects, in elevated style, often invoking a muse, in medias res openings, and treating events of national or cosmic significance.
items.misconception: Epic means any poem that is very long.
items.random_wrong: Epic means any story with a battle in it.
items.why: Length alone isn't enough. Epic has specific conventions (invocation, elevated diction, heroic subject) inherited from Homer through Virgil to Milton — students reduce it to scale.

items.q: What is the structural framework of The Canterbury Tales?
items.correct: A frame narrative — pilgrims travelling to Canterbury tell stories along the way, allowing Chaucer to juxtapose different voices, genres and social classes.
items.misconception: It is one continuous story told by Chaucer from beginning to end.
items.random_wrong: It is a religious text used in Catholic services.
items.why: Students miss the frame structure. Chaucer's strategy is polyphony — voicing many speakers to compare and contrast — not a single narrative voice.

items.q: When was Bradley's Shakespearean Tragedy published?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1904. “1898” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Iago. “Cassio” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was King Lear first performed (approximately)?
items.why: The real figure is 1606 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
